title: Church of the Virgin Mary and St. Abanoub
description: A historic church visited by the Holy Family during their flight into Egypt. It houses a well and a limestone kneading trough blessed by the Virgin Mary, along with the relics of St. Abanoub.

title: Remains of the Temple of Onuris-Shu
description: Scattered granite blocks and architectural fragments from the great temple of the war god Onuris-Shu. King Nectanebo II and later Ptolemies contributed to its construction.

title: Hammam of Samannoud (Old Bathhouse)
description: One of the rare surviving functional historical bathhouses in the Delta, showcasing impressive Ottoman hydraulic engineering and Islamic architecture.

title: Archaeological Site of Sais (Sa El-Hagar)
description: The site of the ancient capital of Egypt during the 26th Dynasty. It was the cult center of the goddess Neith, the patroness of Sais.

title: Hammam Faraoun (Baths of Pharaoh)
description: A red-brick massive structure often misidentified as Pharaoh's bath, likely part of the water management system or a bath complex associated with the temple precinct.

title: Kom Rebwa
description: An archaeological mound (Tell) within the Sais ruins containing domestic quarters, workshops, and pottery kilns from the Saite and Ptolemaic periods.

title: The Great Pit (Northern Enclosure)
description: A massive depression marking the original site of the Great Temple of Neith. The limestone walls were quarried away in medieval times, leaving the 'pit' outline.

title: Temple of Isis (Iseum)
description: Known as the 'Luxor of the Delta', this collapsed temple was dedicated to Isis. Its ruins heavily feature massive granite blocks with intricate reliefs of Nectanebo II and Ptolemy II.

title: Mosque of Sayid Al-Badawi
description: The largest mosque in the Delta and a major Sufi shrine. It houses the tomb of the 13th-century mystic Ahmed Al-Badawi and features distinct Mamluk-style architecture.

title: Sabil of Ali Bey Al-Kabir
description: A historic public water fountain (Sabil) built by the Mamluk ruler Ali Bey Al-Kabir. It stands as a testament to the charitable architecture of the Ottoman period in Tanta.

title: Tanta Museum
description: The museum displays thousands of artifacts spanning Pharaonic, Greco-Roman, Coptic, and Islamic eras, largely discovered in the Delta region sites like Sais and Behbeit El Hagar.

title: Church of Mar Girgis (St. George)
description: The main cathedral of Tanta, a significant spiritual center for the Coptic Orthodox community in Gharbia, known for its beautiful modern Coptic architecture.

title: Mosque of Sidi Ibrahim El-Desouki (Mahalla Branch)
description: A significant local mosque dedicated to the Sufi saint Ibrahim El-Desouki (whose main shrine is in Desouk), serving as a spiritual center in Mahalla.

title: Mosque of Al-Metwally
description: One of the oldest and most fortified-looking mosques in Mahalla, located on high ground (a 'Tell'). It played a role in the city's defense during historic rebellions.

title: Al-Omari Big Mosque (Al-Masjid Al-Kabir)
description: The 'Big Mosque' of Mahalla, historically linked to the Islamic conquest (Amr ibn al-As), though the current structure dates to later Islamic periods. It contains ancient columns.

title: Khoja Mosque
description: A distinctive Ottoman-style mosque in Mahalla, known for its minaret and the funding by a wealthy merchant (Khoja) class prominent in the textile city.

title: Torah Synagogue of Mahalla
description: A historical synagogue comprising one of the few remaining Jewish heritage sites in the Delta, reflecting the once-thriving Jewish community of the industrial city.

title: Zifta Barrage
description: A distinct regulator on the Damietta Branch of the Nile. It is not only an irrigation marvel but a symbol of the 'Republic of Zifta' independence declaration in 1919.

title: Archaeological Mound of Abusir Bana
description: The site of Ancient Djedu (Busiris), the mythological center of the God Osiris in Lower Egypt. While mostly a mound today, it is of immense archaeological and religious significance.

title: Mosque of Sidi Hamza
description: One of the historic mosques in the heart of Samannoud, featuring traditional Delta Islamic architecture.

title: El-Haggar Mosque
description: A historic mosque in the old quarter of Mahalla known for its intricate woodwork and local significance.

title: Church of St. Rebecca (Rafqa) and Her Sons
description: Located in the village of Sunbat, this church holds the relics of St. Rebecca and her five sons, making it a major pilgrimage destination during her feast.

title: Ali Bey Al-Kabir Mosque
description: A mosque commissioned by the autonomous Mamluk ruler Ali Bey Al-Kabir, reflecting the power and patronage of his turbulent reign.

title: Palace of the Khedive (Tanta University Admin)
description: An architectural gem from the Khedival era, now serving as an administrative building, showcasing the European-influenced style of the time.

title: Tanta Railway Station
description: One of the oldest and busiest railway nodes in the Delta, the station building itself is a landmark of Egypt's early railway history.
